articles.title: Car crashes into Amor restaurant in Bamford, causing damage
articles.excerpt: A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, leaving the building's glass front shattered. No injuries were reported.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 13 July 2026 at 21:00. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, leaving the building's glass front shattered. No injuries were reported.

Latest update

A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, July 13, leaving the building's glass front shattered. The incident occurred around midday, prompting an emergency response that included the Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service and Greater Manchester Police.

Pictures shared online showed the damage, with fire service tape cordoning off the area. The restaurant announced it would be closed for the evening due to the incident, stating on social media, "Due to unforeseen circumstances we will be closed tonight."

A spokesperson for Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service confirmed that firefighters were called to the scene shortly after 12.15pm. One fire engine from Rochdale station attended the incident, which involved a car crashing into the commercial building on Martlet Avenue. Fire crews worked to make the area safe before handing the scene over to police.

Fortunately, there were no reports of injuries following the crash. Greater Manchester Police were approached for a statement regarding the incident.

Earlier developments

A car crashed into a restaurant in Bamford, causing significant damage to the front of the building. The incident occurred on Sunday evening, and emergency services were called to the scene shortly after.

Witnesses reported that the vehicle collided with the restaurant, shattering windows and damaging the entrance. Fortunately, no injuries have been reported as a result of the crash.

Local authorities are assessing the damage and the cause of the incident. The restaurant is currently closed while repairs are carried out, and further investigations are ongoing.

Motorists in the area may experience disruptions as recovery efforts continue, and diversions may be in place until the scene is cleared.

Update timeline

  • 13 July 2026, 21:00: Car crashes into Amor restaurant in Bamford, causing damage
  • 13 July 2026, 21:00: Car crashes into restaurant in Bamford, causing significant damage

articles.title: Car Crashes into Restaurant in Rochdale, Causing Significant Damage
articles.excerpt: A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, leaving the building damaged but with no reported injuries.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 13 July 2026 at 21:00. 3 source updates have been combined into this article.

A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, leaving the building damaged but with no reported injuries.

Latest update

A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday (July 13), causing significant damage to the building. The incident occurred around midday, prompting an emergency response that included the Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service and Greater Manchester Police.

Pictures shared online showed the glass front of the restaurant completely shattered, with fire service tape cordoning off the area following the collision. The restaurant announced it would be closed for the evening due to the incident, stating on social media: "Due to unforeseen circumstances we will be closed tonight."

A spokesperson for Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service confirmed that fire crews were called to the scene just after 12.15pm. One fire engine from Rochdale station attended the incident, where firefighters worked to make the area safe before handing the scene over to police. Fire crews remained on site for approximately one hour.

Fortunately, there were no reports of injuries resulting from the crash. Greater Manchester Police have been approached for further comment on the incident.

Earlier developments

A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, leaving the building damaged but with no reported injuries.

A car crashed into a restaurant in Rochdale, causing significant damage to the front of the building. The incident occurred on Tuesday morning, leaving the restaurant's exterior shattered.

Emergency services attended the scene, but no injuries have been reported. The driver of the car is believed to have lost control before colliding with the restaurant.

Local authorities are assessing the damage, and the restaurant is expected to remain closed for repairs. The incident has raised concerns about road safety in the area.

A spokesperson for Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service confirmed that fire crews were called to the scene just after 12.15pm. One fire engine from Rochdale station attended the incident, which involved a car crashing into the commercial building. Firefighters worked to make the area safe before handing the scene over to police. They remained on site for approximately an hour.

There were no reports of injuries following the crash. Greater Manchester Police were also approached for a statement regarding the incident.

A car crashed into a restaurant in Rochdale on 15 July 2026, resulting in significant damage to the front of the building. Specific details about the time of the incident were not provided.

Emergency services responded to the scene, and no injuries have been reported. The extent of the damage suggests that repairs will be necessary before the restaurant can reopen.

articles.title: Junior parkrun for children to take place at Queen’s Park
articles.excerpt: A junior parkrun event for children aged 4 to 14 will be held at Queen’s Park in Rochdale this Sunday at 9am, featuring a 2k run, walk, skip or jump.
articles.content_html:
A junior parkrun event will take place at Queen’s Park in Rochdale this Sunday, July 12, at 9am. The event is open to children aged 4 to 14 and will feature a 2k run, walk, skip or jump.

Participants are encouraged to enjoy the outdoors and engage in physical activity in a supportive environment. The event aims to promote fitness and fun among young people.

Parents and guardians are welcome to accompany their children during the event. Registration is not required, and all abilities are welcome to join in.

This initiative is part of a broader effort to encourage healthy lifestyles among children in the community. For more information, visit the parkrun website.
